---
title: 'Congratulations?'
date: '2016-07-10T16:36:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '2722481156886509084'
blogger_filename: '/2016/07/congratulations.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Armchair Theology'
  - 'Spiritual Growth'
---

<br /><br /><h3>Can I use the word "Congratulate" in reference to someone who is happy about their decision, but I disagree with it?</h3><br />If a person I meet is (in my opinion) about to take the wrong job, marry the wrong person, or make another choice that they see as good, right, and happy, but I know will bring them pain later... can I really be happy for them, or congratulate them?<br /><br />That was the question my wife and I pondered the other night. So I looked up the meaning of the word "Congratulate".<br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i><a href="http://www.etymonline.com/index.php?term=congratulation">congratulation (n.)</a> <a href="http://dictionary.reference.com/search?q=congratulation"><img src="http://www.etymonline.com/graphics/dictionary.gif" /></a>mid-15c., from Latin congratulationem (nominative congratulatio), noun of action from past participle stem of congratulari "wish joy," from com-"together, with" (see <a href="http://www.etymonline.com/index.php?term=com-&amp;allowed_in_frame=0">com-</a>) + gratulari "give thanks, show joy," from gratus "agreeable" (see <a href="http://www.etymonline.com/index.php?term=grace&amp;allowed_in_frame=0">grace</a> (n.)).</i></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i><a href="http://www.dictionary.com/browse/congratulate?s=t" target="_blank">Congratulate</a>&nbsp;(Dictionary.Com)</i><i>verb (used with object), congratulated, congratulating.</i><i>1. to express pleasure to (a person), as on a happy occasion: They congratulated him on his marriage.</i><i>2. Archaic. to express sympathetic joy or satisfaction at (an event).</i><i>3. Obsolete. to salute.</i></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i><a href="https://1828.mshaffer.com/d/word/congratulate">Congratulate</a> 1828.mshaffer.com<br />CONGRATULATE, v.t. [L., grateful, pleasing. See Grace.] To profess ones pleasure or joy to another on account of an event deemed happy or fortunate, as on the birth of a child, success in an enterprise, victory, escape from danger, &amp;c.; to wish joy to another. We congratulate the nation on the restoration of peace.<br /><br />Formerly this verb was followed by to. The subjects of England may congratulate to themselves. But this use of to is entirely obsolete. The use of with after this verb, I congratulate with my country, is perhaps less objectionable, but is rarely used. The intransitive sense of the verb may therefore be considered as antiquated, and no longer legitimate.</i></blockquote><br />The word is build from parts. Con is originally "Com" as in&nbsp;"together, with" (see <a href="http://www.etymonline.com/index.php?term=com-&amp;allowed_in_frame=0">com-</a>) + Gratulari "give thanks, show joy". I am coming together with you, and joining you in your joy.<br /><div><br /></div><div>If I disagree with your decision, I can't really do that. If I truly believe your decision will bring you pain and not joy long term, I will be obligated to either be silent, or warn you of impending danger.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>So, no. I cannot congratulate you on your choice.</div><div><br /></div><div>What can I do, without ruining our relationship/friendship?&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>I can pray you are filled with the wisdom of God, and through that wisdom that you find Shalom (nothing missing, nothing broken, everything (inducing your decision making) whole and complete).<br /><br />So when presented with a card celebrating your marriage to a person you shouldn't have married... I'll simply write "May you be filled with the Wisdom and find Shalom"... or maybe just "Shalom! DW".&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The wonderful part of that, is I can write this on any card, even those I do agree with. It's good for the whole family!<br /><br /><div><div style="text-align: center;">********</div><br /></div><div>By <a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts">Darrell Wolfe</a>&nbsp;| #<a href="http://ctt.ec/5xc67">BeStillBeLed</a></div><div><br />Story Teller | Creative | <a href="http://www.16personalities.com/infj-personality">INFJ</a> | <a href="https://www.gallupstrengthscenter.com/Purchase/en-US/Product?Path=Clifton%20StrengthsFinder">Intellection</a> | Learner | Ideation | Achiever | Input | Multipotentialite<br /><br />New here?<br /><br /><ul><li><a href="http://darrellwolfe.blogspot.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">Start Here</a> for more information.</li><li><a href="http://darrellwolfe.blogspot.com/p/q.html" target="_blank">Ask </a>me anything</li><li><a href="http://darrellwolfe.blogspot.com/p/got-freedom.html" target="_blank">Got LIFE? Not sure, click HERE to find out.</a></li><li>See <a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/by-darrell.html" target="_blank">DarrellWolfe.com</a> for more of my sites.</li></ul><div><br /></div></div></div>
